name: TEA Planning Workflow (Risk → Strategy → Design)
description: Comprehensive test planning workflow combining risk assessment, test strategy, and test design phases. Adaptive complexity-based skipping for simple projects.

phases.name: Business Risk Assessment
phases.description: Identify and analyze business risks (probability × impact)

phases.name: Risk Prioritization
phases.description: Combine and prioritize all identified risks

phases.name: Risk Mitigation Strategies
phases.description: Define strategies to mitigate identified risks

phases.name: Test Scope Definition
phases.description: Define what to test and what NOT to test

phases.name: Test Type Selection
phases.description: Select appropriate test types (unit, integration, E2E, performance, security)

phases.name: Test Environment Planning
phases.description: Define test environment requirements (dev, staging, prod)

phases.name: Test Data Strategy
phases.description: Plan test data generation, mocking, and privacy

phases.name: Quality Metrics Definition
phases.description: Define quality metrics (coverage, defect density, velocity)

phases.name: Test Scenario Identification
phases.description: Identify test scenarios (happy path, edge cases)

phases.name: Negative Testing Design
phases.description: Design negative tests (error handling, invalid input)

phases.name: Test Case Creation
phases.description: Create detailed test cases (preconditions, steps, expected results)

steps.name: Assess Business Risk
steps.objective: Create business risk matrix (5×5: probability × impact)
instructions: Identify business-critical features and user journeys
Assess probability of failure (1-5 scale)
Assess business impact of failure (1-5 scale)
Create 5×5 risk matrix with color coding (green/yellow/red)
Document top 5 business risks

steps.name: Assess Technical Risk
steps.objective: Create technical risk matrix (complexity, dependencies, unknowns)
instructions: Analyze technical complexity (algorithms, architecture)
Map external dependencies (APIs, databases, services)
Identify technical unknowns (new tech, legacy code)
Create technical risk matrix with severity ratings
Document top 5 technical risks

steps.name: Prioritize All Risks
steps.objective: Combine business and technical risks, prioritize testing focus
instructions: Merge business_risk_matrix and technical_risk_matrix
Calculate combined risk score (business_impact × technical_complexity)
Rank all risks by combined score
Define test priorities (P0/P1/P2/P3)
Allocate testing effort based on risk ranking

steps.name: Define Mitigation Strategies
steps.objective: Create risk mitigation strategies for top risks
instructions: For each high-priority risk, define mitigation strategy
Specify testing techniques (unit, integration, E2E, etc.)
Define acceptance criteria for risk mitigation
Estimate effort required for each mitigation
Create action plan with owners and deadlines

steps.name: Define Test Scope
steps.objective: Define what to test and what NOT to test
instructions: List all features/modules in scope for testing
Explicitly list out-of-scope items (3rd party libs, mocks, etc.)
Define test boundaries (where testing starts/stops)
Identify assumptions and dependencies
Document scope limitations and constraints

steps.name: Select Test Types
steps.objective: Select appropriate test types and their coverage targets
instructions: Define test pyramid (unit → integration → E2E ratios)
Specify coverage targets for each test type
Select specialized tests (performance, security, accessibility)
Create test type matrix with rationale for each type
Document tool/framework recommendations per type

steps.name: Plan Test Environments
steps.objective: Define test environment requirements (dev, staging, prod)
instructions: Define environment topology (local, CI, staging, prod-like)
Specify infrastructure requirements (CPU, memory, storage)
Document environment configuration (env vars, secrets, configs)
Define data refresh and reset strategies
Create environment provisioning checklist

steps.name: Plan Test Data Strategy
steps.objective: Plan test data generation, mocking, and privacy
instructions: Define test data sources (production snapshots, synthetic, mocks)
Create data generation strategy (factories, fixtures, randomization)
Plan data privacy and anonymization (GDPR, PII handling)
Define data versioning and reset procedures
Document data dependencies and prerequisites

steps.name: Define Quality Metrics
steps.objective: Define quality metrics (coverage, defect density, velocity)
instructions: Define code coverage targets (line, branch, function)
Specify defect density targets (bugs per 1000 LOC)
Define velocity metrics (tests per sprint, automation rate)
Set quality gates (min coverage, max critical bugs)
Create metrics dashboard specification

steps.name: Identify Test Scenarios
steps.objective: Identify test scenarios (happy path, edge cases)
instructions: List all happy path scenarios (expected user flows)
Identify edge cases (boundary conditions, rare inputs)
Document error scenarios (invalid input, network failures)
Create scenario-to-requirement traceability matrix
Prioritize scenarios by risk and business value

steps.name: Design Boundary Tests
instructions: Identify all input boundaries (min, max, empty, null)
Design tests for boundary-1, boundary, boundary+1
Test numeric ranges (0, 1, MAX_INT, negative)
Test string lengths (empty, 1 char, max length, overflow)
Create boundary test matrix with expected results

steps.name: Design Equivalence Partition Tests
instructions: Identify input domains (ranges, categories, states)
Partition each domain into equivalence classes
Select representative value from each partition
Design tests for valid and invalid partitions
Create partition-to-test-case mapping

steps.name: Design Negative Tests
steps.objective: Design negative tests (error handling, invalid input)
instructions: Identify all error conditions (invalid input, network failures)
Design tests for each error path
Verify error messages are user-friendly and actionable
Test error recovery and rollback mechanisms
Document expected error codes and messages

steps.name: Create Test Cases
steps.objective: Create detailed test cases (preconditions, steps, expected results)
instructions: For each test scenario, create detailed test case
Specify preconditions (setup, prerequisites)
Document step-by-step test procedure
Define expected results and acceptance criteria
Tag test cases (smoke, regression, sanity, etc.)

steps.name: Create Test Strategy Document
instructions: Consolidate all planning artifacts into single document
Structure document: scope, approach, resources, schedule, risks
Add executive summary and recommendations
Include all matrices, checklists, and diagrams
Format for stakeholder review and sign-off

success_criteria: All high-priority risks identified and mitigated
Test scope clearly defined with explicit exclusions
All test cases traceable to requirements
Test strategy document complete and reviewed
